snippets.label: lca_methodological_issue_like_for_like
snippets.text: -237- In the context of whole life energy and carbon, this research has shown that despite international attempts of academics to standardise LCAs, there is still concern behind the consideration of embodied impacts and its inclusion in policy due to methodological issues including data uncertainty. One issues with existing LCA studies identified by the author is that they assume a like-for-like replacement, the case study realities have shown this is unrealistic. Another contribution of this thesis to building adaptation theory is that it has not only highlighted what is considered in adaptation and demolition decisions, it has helped to uncover factors that govern and influence the extent of which thes e decision -making criteria are taken into account (section 9.3) . By conducting case studies in different contexts, it is apparent that different people, planning structures, economic conditions and processes are all influential. The complexity of these issues, including negotiations over planning policies, hierarchies within the planning system, the ability to transfer risk to individuals willing to take it, are not explained by literature focusing on the individual building level as their influence is exacerbated by the increase in scale, both physically and chronologically, provided by the masterplan. This is why the theoretical underpinnings of urban development are applicable to explaining these issues as this

As shown in the professional interviews, there is also a second interpretation which is understanding heritage as character and place-making. 6.2 Whole life energy and carbon The second major benefit of retention which was identified in the academic literature was savings in materials and therefore a reduction in embodied energy and carbon impacts. This section is based on the professional interviews, focus groups and case study interviews to establish whether whole life energy and carbon is a factor currently considered in practice at either the individual or masterplan scales. Figure 6-19 displays the frequency distribution of criteria sitting within the overall theme of environmental factors. It is clear that there are other environmental aspects , beyond energy and carbon, which were discussed during the interviews. These include ground contamination and noise pollution. However, when reviewing these criteria and the relevant quotations, it was clear that for the selected case studies, these were general issues with the masterplan and construction of the development rather than aspects directly affecting adaptation and demolition decisions. Therefore, the following section is limited to those factors
snippets.interpretation_es: Señala la brecha entre literatura y práctica respecto a whole life energy and carbon; sirve para justificar por qué la tesis debe incorporar criterios ambientales explícitos dentro del marco de factibilidad.

snippets.label: masterplan_complexity_breeam
snippets.text: the factors that lead to adaptation versus demolition. A report by the London Assembly (2015, p.4) states “one of the most divisive questions is whether to demolish or refurbish the existing homes”. Examples of decisions made about housing estates are provided within the text but not analysed in detail. Additionally, this report does not include a review of academic literature and the methodology is not clearly outlined. Nevertheless, due to their relevance to the scale of the maste rplan, findings from these reports are referred to in the following chapters. The increased complexity of decision-making at the masterplan level is suggested by the Building Research Establishment’s Environmental Assessment Method (BREEAM) for Communities toolkit (BRE, 2012). This sustainability assessment uses multiple criteria to score a large-scale development for social, economic and environmental impacts. One of the criteria within the ‘resources and energy’ category is ‘existing building and infrastructure’. However, there are only two credits out of 118 available for this category (figure 1-3). The number of issues that are analysed indicates that decision -making at the masterplan level is part of a highly complex system, where a variety of factors are considered and balanced alongside one another (Baker and Moncaster, 2018a). Overall, there is currently limited research focusing on the decision to adapt or demolish existing buildings

Unless stated otherwise, focus of studies is on cultural heritage properties.
3 Likert scale survey: survey respondents rate importance of factors using a scale e.g. 1-5 equates to not important – very important.
Barron Rank-Order: formula to identify single weight (centroid) from combination of weights (Dutta and Husain, 2009).
Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP): technique for hierarchy structuring multiple criteria. Uses pairwise comparison techniques to
interpret ratio of preference between two criteria (Wang and Zeng, 2010).
Delphi technique: consists of three stages: 1) identifying important issues by asking experts; 2) provides opportunity to reconsider
assessment after circulating others opinions; 3) experts propose potential attributes (Kim et al., 2010).
Analytic Network Process (ANP): AHP is starting point and then ANP provides a more generalised model and considered
independencies e.g. connections between different levels of criteria (Wang and Zeng, 2010).

packs.problem_signal: La fuente plantea que las respuestas convencionales del entorno construido frente al cambio climático y la pérdida de biodiversidad son insuficientes y fragmentadas; por ello propone marcos que integren mitigación, adaptación y restauración con resultados ambientales positivos.
packs.method_signal: La investigación usa una argumentación lógica apoyada en revisión de literatura, construcción de principios generalizados, matrices de procesos y servicios ecosistémicos, y prueba de aplicabilidad en un caso urbano existente.
packs.context_signal: Se sitúa en el debate sobre crisis climática, degradación ecosistémica y responsabilidad del entorno construido como principal sitio de actividad cultural y económica, con énfasis en contextos urbanos.
packs.finding_signal: La tesis sostiene que el biomimicry a nivel de ecosistema y el uso de servicios ecosistémicos ofrecen una base más robusta para redefinir metas de desempeño ambiental y localizar puntos de apalancamiento para el cambio en el entorno urbano construido.
packs.limitation_signal: La propia fuente reconoce limitaciones de definición y evidencia cuantitativa en el diseño regenerativo y advierte riesgos del biomimicry ad hoc o superficial, además de señalar límites de alcance y de transferencia directa entre sistemas biológicos y diseño humano.
packs.future_line_signal: Se infiere como línea futura la necesidad de mayor prueba empírica, monitoreo de ejemplos construidos y desarrollo de aplicaciones prácticas en diseño urbano y arquitectónico basadas en servicios ecosistémicos y biomimicry de ecosistemas.
packs.abstract_summary: La tesis investiga si el diseño regenerativo es posible en entornos urbanos y dónde están los principales puntos de apalancamiento para transformar el entorno construido. Para ello examina biomimicry, procesos y servicios ecosistémicos, construye una teoría biomimética de ecosistema aplicable al diseño urbano-arquitectónico y la contrasta con un caso de estudio existente.
packs.pdf_summary: Documento doctoral de gran extensión que construye un marco teórico y metodológico para incorporar biomimicry y servicios ecosistémicos al diseño regenerativo urbano. Su aporte más útil para una investigación aplicada en arquitectura está en criterios, marcos conceptuales, diagramas metodológicos y argumentos para redefinir metas de desempeño ambiental del entorno construido.

pdf_sections.abstract: La fuente argumenta que los edificios y ciudades deben pasar de reducir impactos a generar beneficios ambientales positivos mediante diseño regenerativo, biomimicry y servicios ecosistémicos.
pdf_sections.methodology: Se menciona un enfoque de argumentación lógica, revisión comparada, elaboración de principios generalizados y representación mediante matrices y diagramas organizacionales.
pdf_sections.results: Se reporta la formulación de principios ecosistémicos generalizados, matrices de relaciones y ejercicios de ranking para ubicar servicios ecosistémicos clave en el entorno construido.
pdf_sections.conclusions: null","limitations":"Se delimitan alcance y limitaciones del estudio y se advierte falta de evidencia cuantitativa suficiente en el campo, así como riesgos de enfoques biomiméticos superficiales.
pdf_sections.limitations: Se delimitan alcance y limitaciones del estudio y se advierte falta de evidencia cuantitativa suficiente en el campo, así como riesgos de enfoques biomiméticos superficiales.
pdf_sections.future_work: No aparece una sección explícita visible, pero el texto apunta a profundizar evidencia empírica, monitoreo y aplicaciones prácticas del marco en diseño urbano-arquitectónico.

snippets.text: 54 This would at the same time restore or create ecosystem services and add to resilience (Chapin et al., 2000, Rands et al., 2010). Many current climate change mitigation and adaptation strategies can be described as ‘sustainable’. This is an impr ovement on design that does not consider environmental impacts at all however ‘sustainable’ design ultimately still results in negative environmental impact (Reed, 2007) . Through incremental steps the ultimate goal of such an approach is neutral environmen tal impact as demonstrated in figure 6. Until this goal is reached, the built environment continues to degrade the ecosystems and climate humans are dependent upon for well - being, wealth, and basic survival . Under many current definitions s ustainable design seeks to minimise pollution rather than achieving clean air, soil and water. It minimises energy use, rather than us ing energy from non -damaging renewable sources. It minimises waste rather than eliminating it altogether by creating positive cycles of re source use (McDonough and Braungart, 2002 , page 62 ). Within this paradigm, no attempt is made to remediate past damage. Most urban environments are built in such a way that th e outcome is detrimental to climate, ecosystems, and to people (Newman, 2006), rather than nearing even approximate ‘sustainability’, even if the original meaning of the term (to continue indefinitely) is implied. The goals of creating neutral environmental outcomes in terms of energy use,

snippets.label: metodologia_argumentacion_logica
snippets.text: 66 A logical argumentation project is often set within a larger ‘transcendental venue’ such as ‘nature’, ‘the machine’, or ‘morality’ (Groat a nd Wang, 2002 , page 303 ). This thesis sees ‘nature’ as a system or model which works well within the planet’s ecological operational limitations and functions in a way which is potentially useful for human s to learn from . The logical groundwork for living within ecosystems without degrading them and regenerating them is assumed to be found within understanding ecosystems and then translating this knowledge into a human context. This philosophical stance is discussed further in section 2.2.4. 1.7.2 STAGES OF LOGICAL ARGUMENTATION THROUGHOUT THE THESIS A logical argumentation approach implies that the project has the following traits: broad systemic applicability, paradigmatic innovation, and testability (Groat and Wang, 2002, pages 308-311). The rest of the thesis is divided in to parts following these traits66. Part one of the thesis is a primary logical argumentation treatise because it argues for a new way of conceiving the built environment (broad systemic applicability) and then provides analytic framewo rks and matrix tools (paradigmatic innovation). Primary logical systems can be described as ‘innovative’ because they work at the level of paradigm discourse. A paradigm exists within a cultural and historical context, so most primary logical systems have connections to previously expressed

snippets.text: 95 The issues explored in this section do not mean that human designers cannot learn anything from studying how biology or ecosystems work to become more able to live within them without negative effects, but findings suggest that there is a need both to explore and respect solutions seen in the living world along with tested and successful strategies that humans have devised , and to understand the differences between them. This is important in order to make decisions about what is and is not appropriate in terms of design , so that sustainability is increased , and so that the greater philosophical or ethical implications of biomimicry -for-sustainability are not ignored. 2.4 APPROACHES TO EM PLOYING BIOMIMICRY Through a comparative literature review, and an examination of existing biomimetic technologies, this section defines distinct approaches to biomi metic design. As part of using a critical theory research methodology, terms for these distinctive approaches have been established. Approaches to biomimicry as a design process typically fall into two categories. The first is termed here: ‘design referencing biology’. This is when a human need or design problem is defined first, and then the ways other organisms or ecosystems solve this is explored. snippets.label: genius_of_place_y_sitio_especifico
snippets.text: 117 objective is the we llbeing of ecosystems and people, rather than ‘power, prestige or profit’. Ecomimicry is a kind of biomimicry where flora and fauna of a particular place are studied to find technologies or m ethods that will fit best to the unique conditions of the site. This is because they are most likely to be optimally suited to a particular place because they have evolved to work within a given set of climatic conditions and local resource constraints (Marshall, 2007b) . This is similar to the ‘Genius of Place’ idea fro m the Biomimicry Guild where local biology or ecology is studied to find solutions for site specific design challenges (Peters, 2011, Biomimicry Guild, 2011). Other design ideas or strategies related to ecosystems and architecture are discussed in chapter three. The most important advantage of such an approach to biomimetic design may be the potential positive effects on overall environmental performance. Bio-inspired buildings are likely to have better sustainability outcomes if they mimic the process strategies (how they work) and/or the functions (what they do) of ecosystems, rather than simply the form or material properties of individual organisms (Reap et al., 2005). Ecosystem b iomimicry can operate at both a metaphoric level and a functional level. At a metaphoric level, general ecosystem processes (based on how most ecosystems work) are able to be applied by designers with little ecological

snippets.label: prioridad_biomimicry_ecosistema
snippets.text: 145 3.0 CHAPTER THREE: E XAMINING ECOSYSTEM P ROCESS ES FOR A BUILT ENVIRONMENT CO NTEXT ‘It is clear that industrial systems and biologic al systems are deeply similar. I ndeed it is clear that both are complex systems, and that in ge neral, they evolve to maintain themselves over time without discontinuous change… C onsiderable care must be taken not to let attractive similarities blind one to significant differences, especially where an understanding of those differences can be equally valuable.’ (Allenby and C ooper, 1994) . 3.1 INTRODUCTION Mimicking ecosystems, rather than aspects of individual organisms , is important in biomimicry that seeks to increase the sustainability of the built environment and contribute to a regenerative de sign paradigm. In chapter two it was established that ecosystem biomimicry at the process or function level may have greater potential for long term effective GHG mitigation and adaptation to climate change impacts, while enabling the built environment to become an agent of ecosystem regeneration and a provider of ecosystem services. This chapter will explore this finding further and will build upon the framework presented in chapter two, by examining and defining ecosystem biomimicry at the process level i n greater depth. Ecosystem biomimicry at the function level will be examined in chapter four. This and the subsequent chapter aim to provide an integrated set of principles that could form the theoretical

snippets.interpretation_es: Sostiene que imitar ecosistemas, más que organismos individuales, tiene mayor potencial para un paradigma regenerativo. Es útil para el marco teórico de sostenibilidad urbana sistémica.

snippets.label: principios_ecosistemicos_y_matriz
snippets.text: 146 The methodology used to devise the generalised ecosystem principles is described, and each principle is briefly elaborated. The results are presented as both a simplified list and an organisational matrix diagram where the relationships between principles become apparent. Those aspects of ecosystem processes that are particularly relevant to climate change adaptation or mitigation are identified in order to form an understanding of how ecosystem based biomimicry could be harnessed to address climate change and biodiversity loss impacts on the built environment. Ecosystem processes have not before been mapped to demonstrate relationships and hierarchies, p roviding an overall view of how ecosystems work, in an architectural or design context. The significance of such research is that it provides a thorough and comprehensive basis for the development of biomimicry for architecture and urban environments seeki ng to move into highly sustainable or potentially regenerative paradigms. The research presented here aims to move attempts to mimic ecosystems from the shallow, and misunderstood (Kibert et al., 2002, page 41, Paton, 1992, Korhonen, 2001) , to the more ins ightful, meaningful, and measurable levels that may be possible when knowledge from ecology and biology is thoroughly integrated into architectural design (Birkeland, 2008, page 17 , Gebeshuber et al., 2009). 3.2 ECOLOGY, ECOSYST EMS AND HUMANS

The results are presented as both a simplified list and an organisational matrix diagram where the relationships between principles become apparent. Those aspects of ecosystem processes that are particularly relevant to climate change adaptation or mitigation are identified in order to form an understanding of how ecosystem based biomimicry could be harnessed to address climate change and biodiversity loss impacts on the built environment. Ecosystem processes have not before been mapped to demonstrate relationships and hierarchies, p roviding an overall view of how ecosystems work, in an architectural or design context. The significance of such research is that it provides a thorough and comprehensive basis for the development of biomimicry for architecture and urban environments seeki ng to move into highly sustainable or potentially regenerative paradigms. The research presented here aims to move attempts to mimic ecosystems from the shallow, and misunderstood (Kibert et al., 2002, page 41, Paton, 1992, Korhonen, 2001) , to the more ins ightful, meaningful, and measurable levels that may be possible when knowledge from ecology and biology is thoroughly integrated into architectural design (Birkeland, 2008, page 17 , Gebeshuber et al., 2009). 3.2 ECOLOGY, ECOSYST EMS AND HUMANS
snippets.interpretation_es: Es uno de los chunks más fuertes: explica la metodología para derivar principios ecosistémicos, su representación en lista y matriz, y su relevancia para adaptación/mitigación. Puede inspirar la construcción de una matriz de criterios de factibilidad para adaptive reuse.

snippets.text: environment is the motivation for incorporating an understanding of ecosystems into design, then it is th e outcomes, or functions of ecosystems, that should be the focus , not specifically how ecosystems work. Mimicking the processes of ecosystems could potentially result in better sustainability outcomes (Gebeschuber et al., 2009) but the danger exists that s uch efforts may remain at a shallow level. For example, a development that cycles matter, gathers and uses energy effectively and is able to adapt to changing conditions might be based upon an understanding of ecosystem processes. It may not have environme ntal performance outcomes that are any better overall than other ‘sustainable’ buildings or even conventional ones. Mimicking the 152 Fashion trends that result in the use of extra energy or materials and lead to waste due to demolitions to create them or when they become outmoded (Brand, 1994) rather than functionally obsolete, as is currently the case, would be prevented by a thorough ecosystem biomimicry approach because such practices would be specifically excluded from any ecosystem based and/or regenerative approach to design.

Understanding ecosystem services fits well into the philosophy of encouraging people to move beyond the typical ‘reduce, recycle, efficiency’ sustainability paradigm (Reed, 2007, McDonough and Braungart, 2002) , and to grasp the possible potential for increased ecological and human health when adopting a regenerative approach. A key benefit of mimicking the functi ons of ecosystems is that through careful urban planning, buildings as part of a larger system that are able to mimic natural processes and functions in their creation, use , and eventual end of life, could have the potential to be more resilient and adapt more readily to climate change (TEEB, 2011b). The Econom ics of Ecosystems and Biodiversity group (TEEB, 2011b) provide clear reasons why an understanding of ecosystem service s should be integrated into urban level decision making. Firstly, when the benefits derived from local ecosystems become apparent and are visible to local residents these are more valued and perhaps therefore preserved. For example, understanding that purer water is a result of nearby forests in a particular city could mean it is easier to convince people of the need to conserve the forest 171. This has the potential to contribute to prioritising or preventing certain urban development projects in particular areas, and therefore to long term effect ive spatial planning, providing the overall values of society change to allow this. Secondly, the i mpacts of decision

packs.problem_signal: La fuente plantea que, pese a sus beneficios históricos, ambientales, sociales y económicos, la reutilización adaptativa no suele priorizarse y existe una limitación en la forma de pensar los edificios existentes; además, el desafío no es solo retener y actualizar un edificio, sino hacerlo de forma sostenible y compatible con exigencias contemporáneas.
packs.method_signal: El estudio usa una revisión crítica de conocimiento y práctica sobre adaptive reuse y sostenibilidad para informar un proyecto de diseño aplicado; incorpora análisis de sitio, edificio y contexto, definición de programa, estrategias de intervención y un proceso iterativo de exploración y reevaluación de alternativas.
packs.context_signal: El caso se sitúa en Nueva Zelanda, especialmente en Auckland/Grey Lynn, y discute el rezago relativo de la adopción de adaptive reuse en ese contexto frente a su reconocimiento internacional como estrategia viable de desarrollo sostenible.
packs.finding_signal: La fuente sostiene que adaptive reuse y sostenibilidad se refuerzan mutuamente; la revisión y el caso de diseño sugieren que un edificio existente puede reconvertirse y ampliarse para ofrecer desempeño y valor comparables a obra nueva, conservando energía incorporada y memoria del lugar.
packs.limitation_signal: La fuente reconoce que la sostenibilidad es un concepto amplio, difícil de definir y con alta imprecisión respecto a sus medios de logro; también diferencia su enfoque integral de la mera 'rehabilitación verde' o mejora aislada de componentes.
packs.future_line_signal: Se identifica potencial de investigación futura en la formulación de un marco de toma de decisiones y en el desarrollo detallado de aspectos derivados del proyecto, lo que es transferible a marcos de factibilidad para reconversión y sobre-elevación.
packs.abstract_summary: La investigación propone integrar adaptive reuse y sostenibilidad para maximizar beneficios arquitectónicos, sociales y ambientales. Combina revisión crítica y proyecto de diseño aplicado sobre la reconversión de un edificio comercial existente en Auckland, buscando mostrar que la reutilización adaptativa sostenible es una alternativa viable y socialmente responsable frente a la demolición.
packs.pdf_summary: Tesis/proyecto de arquitectura con dos componentes: uno escrito de revisión crítica sobre adaptive reuse y sostenibilidad, y otro de diseño aplicado sobre la reconversión de un edificio comercial existente. Desarrolla definiciones, beneficios, barreras, integración de sostenibilidad, análisis del sitio y del edificio, estrategias de intervención, estructura, envolvente, servicios y una conclusión con líneas futuras.

pdf_sections.abstract: La fuente argumenta que adaptive reuse y sostenibilidad tienen mayor potencial cuando se aplican en conjunto, y evalúa esa integración mediante revisión crítica y un caso de redesarrollo de uso mixto en un edificio comercial existente.
pdf_sections.methodology: La metodología combina revisión crítica de literatura y práctica con un componente de diseño; incluye análisis de sitio, edificio y contexto, definición programática y desarrollo de estrategias de intervención mediante un proceso reflexivo e iterativo.
pdf_sections.conclusions: La conclusión sostiene que la reutilización adaptativa es una alternativa viable a la demolición y que la integración con sostenibilidad puede producir resultados equivalentes o superiores a la obra nueva en desempeño y calidad espacial, conservando energía y memoria.
pdf_sections.limitations: Se reconoce la amplitud e imprecisión del concepto de sostenibilidad y la dificultad de definir criterios universales para su logro.
pdf_sections.future_work: Se propone profundizar en la formulación de un marco de toma de decisiones y en desarrollos detallados posteriores al proyecto.

snippets.label: abstract_integracion_reuso_sostenibilidad
snippets.text: ABSTRACT Whilst both adaptive reuse and sustainability are important architectural strategies in their own right, greater potential lies in their combined application, facilitating an architectural solution with the benefits of both. This research project is founded in the integration of sustainable practice in adaptive reuse and consists of two components – writing and design. The writing component explores adaptive reuse and sustainability through a critical review of current knowledge and practice, proposes a shift in thinking that takes account of both strategies in combination; and describes and evaluates their application in the mixed‐use redevelopment of an existing commercial building in the Auckland city fringe‐suburb of Grey Lynn. The design component illustrates and makes explicit – in this specific redevelopment situation, and in detail – the processes, applications and implications of adaptively reusing an existing building in a sustainable manner. Every aspect of this research project aims to raise awareness and actively portray the potential of sustainable practice in adaptive reuse as a viable and socially responsible alternative to demolition and replacement.

snippets.text: DIRTY LAUNDRY Page | ix Key Terms Adaptive Reuse – Adaptive reuse is ‘conventionally defined as the process of adapting old structures for new purposes.’1 This is a broad strategy which includes varying levels of intervention from minor change through to commercial redevelopment. This research project primarily focuses on adaptive reuse at the larger end of the scale as a holistic strategy which looks to fulfil contemporary expectations. Sustainability – Sus.tain.able adj 1: capable of being sustained 2 a: of, relating to, or being a method of harvesting or using a resource so that the resource is not depleted or permanently damages <~ techniques> <~ agriculture> b: of or relating to a lifestyle involving the use of sustainable mathods <~ society> ‐ sus.tain.abil.i.ty n (Merriam‐Webster 1994)2 Integration – in∙te∙gra∙tion. noun 1. an act or instance of combining into an integral whole. Within the context of this research project the word integration is used to reference the way in which the processes of adaptive reuse and sustainability can work together to enhance and inform each other resulting in a process of sustainable adaptive reuse. 1 Adaptivereuse.net, "Adaptive Reuse Contemporary Metamorphoses," WordPress, http://adaptivereuse.net/about‐adaptive‐ reuse/. 2 Terry Williamson, Anthony Radford, and Helen Bennetts, Understanding Sustainable Architecture (London: Spon Press, 2003), 3.

snippets.label: significancia_stock_existente_vs_demolicion
snippets.text: DIRTY LAUNDRY Page | 2 Significance of this Project This project addresses a limitation in current thinking within the New Zealand architectural profession. It is an exploration, analysis and evaluation of the practices of both adaptive reuse and sustainability in an effort to raise awareness of their potential as a sustainable development option when utilised together. Our existing building stock plays a fundamental role in our society. It contains large quantities of embodied energy, materials and resources as well as contributing to the streetscape, character and embodied memory of our communities. When buildings no longer meet expectations, demolition is frequently employed, contributing to the building industry as 'the single largest consumer of resources and the single largest contributor to the waste stream.'5 Demolition not only contributes to the waste flow, it squanders previously expended materials, with further energy and resources required to replace the building. It is destructive and results in the loss of a layer of our history forever. Even the most ordinary buildings play a role in the ongoing memory of society, communities and individuals. However, despite this, demolition and replacement is often sought as the primary development solution for a site with an existing building. Whilst it is not uncommon for a building’s use to no longer meet the demands of society, it is

snippets.interpretation_es: Explica por qué el stock construido existente importa y por qué la demolición implica desperdicio de recursos, energía incorporada y memoria urbana. Sirve como base fuerte para el planteamiento del problema y la justificación ambiental-social.

snippets.label: beneficios_barreras_y_necesidad_de_cambio_de_enfoque
snippets.text: DIRTY LAUNDRY Page | 3 extremities is rehabilitation6. Adaptive reuse is a form of rehabilitation and is the focus of this project. It has many historic, environmental, social and economic benefits; all of which make it an essential component of sustainable development. However, despite its benefits and adoption on the world scene, it is slow to be adopted in New Zealand. In order for adoption to increase, there needs to be a shift in our thinking about existing buildings and an education about the potential of what can be achieved with a new approach. Given the sustainable attributes inherent in adaptive reuse the challenge is not only how to retain and upgrade an existing building but also how to do this in a sustainable manner that meets contemporary requirements. The significance of this project is in the intersection of adaptive reuse and sustainability, and the investigation of how they can enhance each other. Whilst there are a variety of publications on adaptive reuse and sustainability separately, there are limited resources available on the intersection of the two. As such, this research project will contribute to a field of architecture which is not currently well acknowledged. Research Question How can an existing building be adaptively reused in an environmentally and socially sustainable manner to achieve a viable development alternative to demolition and replacement?

It has many historic, environmental, social and economic benefits; all of which make it an essential component of sustainable development. However, despite its benefits and adoption on the world scene, it is slow to be adopted in New Zealand. In order for adoption to increase, there needs to be a shift in our thinking about existing buildings and an education about the potential of what can be achieved with a new approach. Given the sustainable attributes inherent in adaptive reuse the challenge is not only how to retain and upgrade an existing building but also how to do this in a sustainable manner that meets contemporary requirements. The significance of this project is in the intersection of adaptive reuse and sustainability, and the investigation of how they can enhance each other. Whilst there are a variety of publications on adaptive reuse and sustainability separately, there are limited resources available on the intersection of the two. As such, this research project will contribute to a field of architecture which is not currently well acknowledged. Research Question How can an existing building be adaptively reused in an environmentally and socially sustainable manner to achieve a viable development alternative to demolition and replacement?
snippets.interpretation_es: Sintetiza beneficios del adaptive reuse, su lenta adopción y la necesidad de un cambio de pensamiento respecto de edificios existentes. Es muy útil para conectar barreras actuales con la necesidad de investigación aplicada.

snippets.label: objetivos_y_metodologia_general
snippets.text: DIRTY LAUNDRY Page | 6 Objectives  To explore, critically analyse and present existing knowledge and practice of adaptive reuse and sustainability.  To discuss the integration of adaptive reuse with sustainable design practice.  To use the findings above to develop a comprehensive design solution whereby an existing building is adaptively reused in a sustainable manner.  To make explicit the process of adaptively reusing a redundant building to aid in the education of others.  To demonstrate the potential of adaptive reuse as a sustainable development option and alternative to demolition and replacement. Methodology This project explores the intersection of two key concepts to inform design; adaptive reuse and sustainability. Whilst these are both areas of long‐standing personal interest, I recognised an increased level of understanding was fundamental to the success of my project. This involved a period of initial research, which guided the design at the outset. The design then informed the need for further research and a cyclic process of research and design evolved. This cyclic process is similar to that of the action research method, which has guided the evolution of this project. Action research originated in the 1940s through the work of Kurt Lewin, with a focus on social research. Since then it has been used across a range of disciplines including architecture, as outlined in Architectural Research Methods.7

snippets.label: adaptive_reuse_como_respuesta_al_cambio_de_uso_y_estandares
snippets.text: DIRTY LAUNDRY Page | 11 Adaptive reuse is the process of adapting old structures for new purposes.10 It has many benefits and has been acknowledged both locally and internationally as a viable development solution for a site with an existing building. Despite this, there are barriers to adaptive reuse, and, as a result, it is often not a development option that is prioritised in New Zealand. Societies change and develop over time and it is not uncommon for a building's use to no longer fit the community in which it stands. The dynamic nature of occupancy means that any building is unlikely to have the same intensity or type of use throughout its serviced life.11 Expectations of buildings, comfort levels and technical standards also change over time, requiring older buildings to undergo change and modernization in order to fulfil contemporary expectations.12 Change is a characteristic inherent in buildings and this is a concept readily addressed in literature by Johannes Cramer and Stefan Breitling13, Luis Fernandez‐Galiano14 and James Douglas.15 Furthermore, Stewart Brand examines buildings in the dimension of time, noting that all buildings are forced to adapt, however some do so more gracefully than others.16 10 Adaptivereuse.net, "Adaptive Reuse Contemporary Metamorphoses." 11 James Douglas, Building Adaptation, Second ed. (Oxford: Butterworth‐Heinemann, 2002), 141.

(Oxford: Butterworth‐Heinemann, 2002), 141.
snippets.interpretation_es: Fundamenta que los edificios cambian de adecuación por transformaciones sociales, programáticas y técnicas, lo que vuelve pertinente la reconversión. Es especialmente útil para argumentar reconversión de activos comerciales obsoletos.

snippets.label: beneficios_ambientales_sociales_y_calidad_del_entorno
snippets.text: DIRTY LAUNDRY Page | 15 Adaptive reuse also provides the opportunity to upgrade our existing buildings and provide more energy efficient and healthy environments. Adaptation work can prioritise sustainable choices to create environments that perform better, are more desirable to live and work in with a conscious approach to both embodied and operating energy. Social Benefits Many sources, including Cramer, acknowledge that the thoughtless demolition of old buildings is now perceived not only as an ecological waste but also as the eradication of local identity, of cultural heritage and of socio‐economic values.28 The retention of existing buildings can have long term benefits for communities, breathing new life into them and assisting in the retention of the local identity and embodied memory. Buildings have a history and are a link with the past; adaptation of these into accessible and usable places often encourages public interaction and initiates an increased interest in the building, attracting visitors enticed by the original and authentic nature. Most major cities have areas where many historic buildings have been adaptively reused, and often these are the most vibrant areas of the city with a mixture of building uses and activities.29 They are seen as authentic and attractive places to live, work and visit, whereas streets lined with modern and contemporary buildings are often sterile, lacking character and community.

snippets.label: integracion_holistica_vs_greening_parcial
snippets.text: DIRTY LAUNDRY Page | 30 Critical Review of Current Practice Currently, sustainable practice is predominantly employed in new construction and adaptive reuse is often not done in a way which prioritises or explores the full potential of sustainable design. A new approach would be to maximise the use of sustainable technologies and design practice in the adaptive reuse of our existing building stock. It is important to note that the combined application of adaptive reuse and sustainability is different from the current trend of greening existing buildings. The proposed concept of integration is a holistic vision to adapt and often fundamentally change an existing building in order to accommodate a new use and provide social, economic and environmental benefits rather than the replacement or upgrading of independent building components or services system in order to reduce energy consumption for economic reasons. Whilst there are numerous adaptive reuse projects and many projects which look to explore sustainability, there is less evidence of sustainable practice in adaptive reuse. ‘Each project, by itself, may have limited impact, but as a means of changing attitudes and values, the possibilities inherent in even very modest projects are immense.’68 Following is a brief description of two projects which are considered to have successfully dealt with the integration of sustainable practice in adaptive reuse.

snippets.interpretation_es: Distingue claramente entre una integración holística de adaptive reuse + sostenibilidad y una simple mejora técnica aislada. Esta distinción es clave para el marco conceptual y para delimitar el alcance de la propuesta aplicada.

snippets.label: criterios_estructurales_y_evaluacion_inicial
snippets.text: DIRTY LAUNDRY Page | 73 DESIGN STRATEGIES Structure In adaptive reuse projects, initial investigations and evaluations are fundamental to establish the construction method, structural integrity and seismic requirements for the existing building. Working with a structurally sound building is advantageous, however, there are many options available for strengthening and seismic upgrading. Techniques are commonly related to the upgrading or re placement of foundations, introducing additional structure such as shear walls or cross bracing to accommodate both seismic and gravity loads, or the strengthening of existing structure through techniques such as “wrapping” existing columns with additional reinforced concrete or carbon fibre.104 Decisions, whilst often linked to costs and efficiency, may also be influenced by aesthetic considerations and whether the intention is to express or conceal structural work. Section 112 of the Building Act (2004) requires that 'for any alteration, the building shall continue to comply with the structural provisions of the Building Code to at le ast the same extent as before the alteration.'105 This means that alteration work cannot weaken the building. Extra building strength would therefore be required where structural elements are to be weakened or if significant additional mass is to be added. In 2005, Holmes Consulting Group, after carrying out a seismic evaluation, produced a report

snippets.label: hallazgos_del_caso_extension_vertical_y_nueva_anexa
snippets.text: the potential of adaptive reuse as a sustainable development option and a viable alternative to demolition and replacement. The resulting adaptively reused and augmented building is, in my opinion, at least the equal – in performance and environment – of a new building that might have replaced it; with th e significant benefits of having conserved energy and preserved memory. The design consists of the existing building, a vertical extension and a new annexe. It involved the research and implementation of an ‘anthology’ of adaptive reuse strategies. The design seeks to actively portray the process of adaptively reusing an existi ng building in a sustainable manner; in a way that clearly distinguishes the new work from the existing. In my opinion, the most significant feature of the new work is the atrium which incorporates both pragmatic and spatial values, significantly enhancing the internal environments. An overall objective of this research proj ect was to raise awareness of adaptive reuse as a viable development strategy and its potential for integration with sustainable design practice. In

It is hoped the research project findings will be made available through pub lication, presentation, and consultancy. All the research project objectives (as stated in the Introduction) have been achieved and following submission of this document, design work will continue for the next three weeks. Whilst the overall intentions have been resolved and will remain unchanged, refinement of design and detailing will continue du ring this time.   "area": "Arquitectura",
  "topic": "Ideas de negocio para contribuir a superar la crisis de vivienda en Canadá mediante la sobre-elevación en mass timber como estrategia de adaptive reuse en edificios comerciales tipo B y C en Toronto.",
  "problem_context": "La crisis de vivienda en Canadá obliga a las ciudades a identificar estrategias de densificación y reconversión de activos subutilizados con mayor rapidez y menor huella de carbono. En Toronto, numerosos edificios comerciales tipo B y C presentan vacancia, obsolescencia funcional y baja rentabilidad, pero podrían convertirse en soporte para soluciones de adaptive reuse y sobre-elevación liviana en mass timber si se demuestra su viabilidad arquitectónica, normativa y de negocio.",
  "research_line": "Arquitectura sostenible, adaptive reuse, regeneración urbana y factibilidad de reconversión habitacional.",
  "target_population": "Edificios comerciales tipo B y C en Toronto, en condiciones de obsolescencia o subutilización, con potencial de reconversión habitacional y sobre-elevación en mass timber mediante estrategias de adaptive reuse.",
  "available_data": "Se asume disponibilidad de tipologías de edificios comerciales obsoletos, precedentes de reconversión de oficinas a vivienda, referencias sobre extensiones verticales en mass timber, criterios de zoning y building code de Toronto, indicadores de demanda de vivienda y marcos de factibilidad arquitectónica y económica.",
  "preferred_methodology": "Enfoque aplicado con análisis tipológico, revisión comparada de precedentes, construcción de criterios de evaluación para adaptive reuse y sobre-elevación en mass timber, y formulación de un marco de factibilidad arquitectónica, regulatoria y de negocio para la reconversión habitacional."
}

Tema:
{
  "topic": "Ideas de negocio para contribuir a superar la crisis de vivienda en Canadá mediante la sobre-elevación en mass timber como estrategia de adaptive reuse en edificios comerciales tipo B y C en Toronto.",
  "problem_context": "La crisis de vivienda en Canadá obliga a las ciudades a identificar estrategias de densificación y reconversión de activos subutilizados con mayor rapidez y menor huella de carbono. En Toronto, numerosos edificios comerciales tipo B y C presentan vacancia, obsolescencia funcional y baja rentabilidad, pero podrían convertirse en soporte para soluciones de adaptive reuse y sobre-elevación liviana en mass timber si se demuestra su viabilidad arquitectónica, normativa y de negocio.",
  "preferred_methodology": "Enfoque aplicado con análisis tipológico, revisión comparada de precedentes, construcción de criterios de evaluación para adaptive reuse y sobre-elevación en mass timber, y formulación de un marco de factibilidad arquitectónica, regulatoria y de negocio para la reconversión habitacional."
}

Estrategia global ya propuesta:
{
  "dominant_methods": [
    "Revision sistematica y revision critica de literatura para mapear factores, vacancia/subuso y marcos de adaptive reuse.",
    "Estudios de caso aplicados y comparados para explorar decisiones de adaptacion, demolicion, rehabilitacion y ampliacion.",
    "Enfoques cualitativos e inductivos/secuenciales para identificar criterios y comprender decisiones en contexto.",
    "Modelos de decision multicriterio (MCDM), especialmente AHP, para jerarquizar criterios y seleccionar alternativas de nuevo uso.",
    "Evaluacion de ciclo de vida y analisis de escenarios para contrastar rehabilitacion/adaptive reuse frente a nueva construccion.",
    "Investigacion por diseño o design-led research para traducir la revision teorica en estrategias de intervencion arquitectonica."
  ],
  "dominant_frameworks": [
    "Framework de adaptive reuse sostenible con enfasis en vacancia/subuso y tipos de intervencion: WBAR, MUML, PAR y STAR.",
    "Integracion adaptive reuse + sostenibilidad como enfoque arquitectonico holistico, no limitado a 'greening' parcial.",
    "Marco MCDM/AHP con criterios arquitectonicos, culturales, sociales, legales, ambientales y economicos.",
    "Enfoque de toma de decisiones multiescalar: edificio individual, conjunto/masterplan y ciclo de vida del activo.",
    "Framework de whole-life carbon / LCA con atencion a limites del sistema, escenarios y comparabilidad metodologica.",
    "Marco complementario de diseno regenerativo/servicios ecosistemicos como capa aspiracional para criterios ambientales ampliados."
  ],
  "key_findings": [
    "Evidencia directa: la literatura sobre adaptive reuse revisada en W4376279888 se concentra principalmente en edificios totalmente vacantes y en intervenciones de edificio completo; la vacancia suele asumirse mas que operacionalizarse.",
    "Evidencia directa: W4376279888 identifica que la vacancia no aparece suficientemente incorporada en herramientas MCDA, pese a ser un motor central del adaptive reuse.",
    "Evidencia directa: W4376279888 propone distinguir tipos de adaptive reuse, incluyendo parcial y mixed-use, lo que es metodologicamente util para edificios comerciales B y C potencialmente subutilizados, no necesariamente vacios por completo.",
    "Evidencia directa: W2234287179 sostiene que adaptive reuse y sostenibilidad tienen mayor potencial cuando se aplican conjuntamente y que las investigaciones iniciales del edificio y su estructura son fundamentales antes de definir estrategias de intervencion.",
    "Evidencia directa: W3127169891 muestra que un modelo AHP puede estructurar y ponderar criterios de seleccion de nuevo uso, con regla explicita de consistencia (CR<0.10) y participacion de actores.",
    "Evidencia directa: W3013726001 muestra que los criterios no son estables ni universales; una misma condicion puede interpretarse como barrera o ventaja segun actor y contexto, y que la decision real es iterativa, no lineal.",
    "Evidencia directa: W3177188553 aporta soporte para justificar adaptive reuse desde reduccion de GEI y conservacion de recursos, pero tambien advierte que los resultados LCA dependen fuertemente de limites del sistema, supuestos y datos de fondo.",
    "Inferencia prudente: para una tesis aplicada en Toronto, el corpus respalda mejor un marco de factibilidad multicriterio y comparado que una afirmacion concluyente de viabilidad economica o normativa local.",
    "Inferencia prudente: la sobre-elevacion en mass timber puede integrarse como alternativa de intervencion dentro de un marco de adaptive reuse, pero el corpus visible no valida por si solo su factibilidad estructural, normativa o financiera en Toronto.",
    "Inferencia prudente: el marco regenerativo/servicios ecosistemicos sirve mejor como complemento de criterios ambientales y urbanos de valor agregado que como nucleo metodologico principal del estudio."
  ],
  "evidence_gaps": [
    "Vacio bloqueante: no hay en el corpus visible evidencia local verificable sobre zoning, building code, fire code, permisos o politicas de conversion residencial en Toronto/Canada.",
    "Vacio bloqueante: no hay evidencia directa del corpus sobre mass timber rooftop additions en edificios comerciales B y C de Toronto ni sobre limites de altura, cargas, fuego, evacuacion o seguros.",
    "Vacio bloqueante: no hay datos locales de mercado para sustentar demanda, rentas, absorcion, CAPEX/OPEX, estructura financiera o modelo de negocio específico.",
    "Vacio importante: no aparece una tipologia local consolidada de edificios comerciales tipo B y C en Toronto con atributos geometricos, estructurales y de vacancia comparables.",
    "Vacio importante: faltan precedentes locales o regionales de oficina/comercial-a-vivienda con sobre-elevacion en madera masiva para triangulacion de criterios.",
    "Vacio importante: no hay protocolo local de evaluacion estructural para capacidad de carga remanente, refuerzo requerido y compatibilidad con sobre-elevacion liviana.",
    "Vacio importante: no hay validacion con actores locales (arquitectos, developers, code consultants, planners, lenders, insurers).",
    "Vacio menor pero relevante: el marco regenerativo no tiene evidencia empirica fuerte en este corpus para convertirse en criterio decisor principal de factibilidad."
  ],
  "proposal_directions": [
    "Adoptar una tesis de enfoque aplicado, no demostrativo-causal, orientada a construir un marco de factibilidad preliminar para reconversion habitacional con sobre-elevacion en mass timber.",
    "Estructurar la metodologia en fases: 1) revision sintesis del corpus y extraccion de criterios; 2) construccion de tipologias/casos-tipo; 3) matriz multicriterio de factibilidad; 4) contraste experto local; 5) aplicacion piloto a uno o varios prototipos.",
    "Tomar la vacancia/subuso como variable explicita del problema y del instrumento, no como supuesto binario.",
    "Distinguir entre escenarios de adaptive reuse parcial, mixto y de edificio completo, porque el corpus sugiere que esa diferencia cambia criterios y decisiones.",
    "Usar AHP o un MCDM simplificado para ponderar criterios, pero solo despues de una preseleccion cualitativa de criterios y alternativas relevantes para el caso.",
    "Incorporar una capa de prefactibilidad de carbono/beneficio ambiental basada en principios de whole-life thinking, evitando afirmaciones cuantitativas duras si no hay datos locales.",
    "Separar claramente factibilidad arquitectonica, regulatoria y de negocio, para que la tesis no dependa de probar cada una con el mismo nivel de evidencia.",
    "Tratar la sobre-elevacion en mass timber como alternativa de diseño-negocio a evaluar, no como solucion ya validada."
  ],
  "proposal_method_candidate": {
    "method_family": "MCDM aplicado con base cualitativa-comparada y validacion experta",
    "research_design": "Diseno secuencial mixto de alcance aplicado: revision sintetica del corpus -> analisis tipologico y de precedentes -> construccion de criterios -> ponderacion multicriterio preliminar (AHP o scoring ponderado) -> validac",
    "application_scope_status": "parcial",
    "candidate_techniques": [
      "Matriz de extraccion de criterios desde literatura priorizada.",
      "Analisis tipologico de edificios comerciales B y C candidatos.",
      "Revision comparada de precedentes de conversion/adaptive reuse y ampliacion vertical.",
      "Construccion de matriz de factibilidad por dimensiones: arquitectonica, regulatoria, ambiental y de negocio.",
      "AHP con comparaciones pareadas para ponderar criterios, sujeto a panel experto.",
      "Scoring multicriterio simplificado cuando no sea viable implementar AHP completo.",
      "Entrevistas semiestructuradas o panel Delphi corto con expertos locales para validar pesos y umbrales.",
      "Analisis de escenarios para alternativas de intervencion: reuse parcial, conversion completa, conversion + sobre-elevacion."
    ],
    "selection_rationale": [
      "El corpus ofrece apoyo directo a los modelos multicriterio y a la estructuracion de decisiones complejas con criterios heterogeneos.",
      "Tambien ofrece apoyo fuerte a un enfoque secuencial e iterativo, mas coherente con la naturaleza no lineal de estas decisiones.",
      "Permite traducir el problema de tesis a una herramienta util para decision preliminar, lo cual tiene alta utilidad academica y profesional.",
      "No exige afirmar causalidad ni contar con base de datos local completa desde el inicio; puede operar como marco tentativo que luego se valida.",
      "Es compatible con el interes del proyecto por criterios de evaluacion, factibilidad y formulacion de ideas de negocio."
    ],
    "evidence_support_level": "medio",

      "Validacion economico-financiera con supuestos de mercado local.",
      "Validacion del panel de criterios y ponderaciones con actores locales.",
      "Prueba piloto en casos reales o prototipos locales con datos consistentes."
    ]

  "proposal_framework_candidate": {

    "supporting_frameworks": [

      "Integracion holistica adaptive reuse + sostenibilidad.",
      "Whole-life thinking / LCA como modulo ambiental de apoyo, no como evidencia concluyente unica.",
      "Marco multiescalar de decision: edificio-activo-conjunto urbano.",
      "Servicios ecosistemicos/regenerative design como modulo optativo de valor urbano-ambiental."
    ],
    "selection_rationale": [

      "W3013726001 ayuda a evitar simplificaciones lineales y a reconocer que los criterios cambian segun escala, actor y contexto de desarrollo.",
      "W3177188553 fortalece la justificacion ambiental y la cautela metodologica respecto a carbono y comparaciones con obra nueva.",
      "W4200361813 puede enriquecer el discurso de valor urbano y regenerativo, pero no conviene sobredimensionarlo metodologicamente en esta tesis."

      "Incorporacion explicita de modulo de negocio con indicadores de mercado y riesgo.",
      "Validacion empírica de pesos, umbrales y reglas de descarte."

  "gap_resolution_plan": {

      "Que la vacancia/subuso debe modelarse como gradiente o condicion diferenciada y no como supuesto binario.",
      "Que los criterios deben agruparse al menos en dimensiones arquitectonicas, regulatorias/legales, ambientales y economicas/de negocio.",
      "Que la decision debe evaluarse por escenarios de intervencion, no solo por una opcion unica."
    ],
    "blocking_gaps": [

      "Datos estructurales y de capacidad de edificios objetivo.",
      "Datos de mercado para el modelo de negocio.",

    "validation_actions": [

      "Incorporar una ola LLM o revision experta dedicada exclusivamente a mass timber additions y requisitos de fire/structure/acoustics/egress.",
      "Construir una ficha de caso-tipo local con variables minimas: ano, luces, modulación, altura libre, sistema estructural, ocupacion, vacancia, lote, zoning.",
      "Realizar 5-10 entrevistas expertas o Delphi corto para validar criterios, pesos y umbrales de descarte.",
      "Preparar una prefactibilidad economica con escenarios y rangos, no con cifras puntuales no verificadas.",
      "Si es posible, aplicar el marco a 1-3 prototipos de edificios para probar consistencia y utilidad."

    "do_not_claim": [

        "caption": "",
        "text": "El articulo se situa en el contexto de cambios recientes en el uso de edificios urbanos, intensificados por COVID-19 y por transformaciones tecnologicas, que incrementaron vacancias y subuso en las ciudades y reabrieron el debate sobre regeneracion urbana, resiliencia y sostenibilidad."
      },

        "quote_hash": null,
        "asset_key": null,
        "asset_path": null,
        "caption": "",
        "text": "El caso se sitúa en Nueva Zelanda, especialmente en Auckland/Grey Lynn, y discute el rezago relativo de la adopción de adaptive reuse en ese contexto frente a su reconocimiento internacional como estrategia viable de desarrollo sostenible."

        "text": "El caso analizado es un antiguo hospital administrativo de Kyrenia, Chipre, edificio patrimonial del periodo britanico que perdio su funcion original, quedo mayormente vacante y requiere una nueva funcion compatible.         "asset_path": null,
        "caption": "",
        "text": "El articulo se situa en el contexto de cambios recientes en el uso de edificios urbanos, intensificados por COVID-19 y por transformaciones tecnologicas, que incrementaron vacancias y subuso en las ciudades y reabrieron el debate sobre regeneracion urbana, resiliencia y sostenibilidad."
      },
      {
        "evidence_id": "signal:ece466c2d64db902",
        "type": "interpreted_signal",
        "source_id": "https://openalex.org/W2234287179",
        "source_title": "Dirty laundry: The adaptive reuse of an existing building",
        "section_keys": [

        "label": "Senal interpretativa de contexto",
        "citation_eligibility": "paraphrase_only",
        "page": null,
        "quote_hash": null,
        "asset_key": null,
        "asset_path": null,
        "caption": "",
        "text": "El caso se sitúa en Nueva Zelanda, especialmente en Auckland/Grey Lynn, y discute el rezago relativo de la adopción de adaptive reuse en ese contexto frente a su reconocimiento internacional como estrategia viable de desarrollo sostenible."
      },
      {
        "evidence_id": "signal:9fbd4f362882c318",

        "page": null,
        "quote_hash": null,
        "asset_key": null,
        "asset_path": null,
        "caption": "",
        "text": "El caso analizado es un antiguo hospital administrativo de Kyrenia, Chipre, edificio patrimonial del periodo britanico que perdio su funcion original, quedo mayormente vacante y requiere una nueva funcion compatible. Aunque el contexto no es Toronto ni reconversion residencial, la fuente es transferible como referencia metodologica para evaluar edificios existentes con obsolescencia funcional y valor urbano."
      },
      {
        "evidence_id": "signal:2857abd8b1de9724",
        "type": "interpreted_signal",
        "source_id": "https://openalex.org/W3013726001",

        "text": "A escala de masterplan, las decisiones sobre adaptar o demoler no dependen solo de atributos físicos del edificio; también intervienen escala relativa dentro del conjunto, flujos peatonales y vehiculares, estructura del sistema de planeamiento, condiciones económicas y poder de actores como autoridades, comunidades y desarrolladores."
      },

        "caption": "",
        "text": "La tesis sugiere ampliar el encuadre teórico de las decisiones de adaptación/demolición mediante modelos compuestos de desarrollo urbano y mejorar el tratamiento metodológico de whole life carbon, especialmente frente a supuestos poco realistas como el reemplazo like-for-like en estudios LCA."

llm_prompts.label: Paso 6 auditoria de trazabilidad
llm_prompts.promptTemplate:
Eres un auditor de trazabilidad academica. Revisa si el paquete consolidado puede pasar a redaccion de secciones.

Reglas:
- responde en espanol
- identifica claims sin evidencia suficiente
- identifica advertencias de trazabilidad
- indica notas concretas para el siguiente lab
- no agregues nuevas fuentes ni nuevos datos
- audita trazabilidad por IDs, elegibilidad y claims; no necesitas re-leer texto completo aqui porque el quality gate deterministico valida existencia de IDs y rutas

Estrategia:
{{STRATEGY_PLAN}}

Dossiers:
{{DOSSIERS}}

Catalogo auditable de evidence units:
{{EVIDENCE_AUDIT_CATALOG}}
llm_prompts.promptText:
Eres un auditor de trazabilidad academica. Revisa si el paquete consolidado puede pasar a redaccion de secciones.

Reglas:
- responde en espanol
- identifica claims sin evidencia suficiente
- identifica advertencias de trazabilidad
- indica notas concretas para el siguiente lab
- no agregues nuevas fuentes ni nuevos datos
- audita trazabilidad por IDs, elegibilidad y claims; no necesitas re-leer texto completo aqui porque el quality gate deterministico valida existencia de IDs y rutas

Estrategia:
{
  "dominant_methods": [
    "Revision sistematica y revision critica de literatura para mapear factores, vacancia/subuso y marcos de adaptive reuse.",
    "Estudios de caso aplicados y comparados para explorar decisiones de adaptacion, demolicion, rehabilitacion y ampliacion.",
    "Enfoques cualitativos e inductivos/secuenciales para identificar criterios y comprender decisiones en contexto.",
    "Modelos de decision multicriterio (MCDM), especialmente AHP, para jerarquizar criterios y seleccionar alternativas de nuevo uso.",
    "Evaluacion de ciclo de vida y analisis de escenarios para contrastar rehabilitacion/adaptive reuse frente a nueva construccion.",
    "Investigacion por diseño o design-led research para traducir la revision teorica en estrategias de intervencion arquitectonica."
  ],
  "dominant_frameworks": [
    "Framework de adaptive reuse sostenible con enfasis en vacancia/subuso y tipos de intervencion: WBAR, MUML, PAR y STAR.",
    "Integracion adaptive reuse + sostenibilidad como enfoque arquitectonico holistico, no limitado a 'greening' parcial.",
    "Marco MCDM/AHP con criterios arquitectonicos, culturales, sociales, legales, ambientales y economicos.",
    "Enfoque de toma de decisiones multiescalar: edificio individual, conjunto/masterplan y ciclo de vida del activo.",
    "Framework de whole-life carbon / LCA con atencion a limites del sistema, escenarios y comparabilidad metodologica.",
    "Marco complementario de diseno regenerativo/servicios ecosistemicos como capa aspiracional para criterios ambientales ampliados."
  ],
  "key_findings": [
    "Evidencia directa: la literatura sobre adaptive reuse revisada en W4376279888 se concentra principalmente en edificios totalmente vacantes y en intervenciones de edificio completo; la vacancia suele asumirse mas que operacionalizarse.",
    "Evidencia directa: W4376279888 identifica que la vacancia no aparece suficientemente incorporada en herramientas MCDA, pese a ser un motor central del adaptive reuse.",
    "Evidencia directa: W4376279888 propone distinguir tipos de adaptive reuse, incluyendo parcial y mixed-use, lo que es metodologicamente util para edificios comerciales B y C potencialmente subutilizados, no necesariamente vacios por completo.",
    "Evidencia directa: W2234287179 sostiene que adaptive reuse y sostenibilidad tienen mayor potencial cuando se aplican conjuntamente y que las investigaciones iniciales del edificio y su estructura son fundamentales antes de definir estrategias de intervencion.",
    "Evidencia directa: W3127169891 muestra que un modelo AHP puede estructurar y ponderar criterios de seleccion de nuevo uso, con regla explicita de consistencia (CR<0.10) y participacion de actores.",
    "Evidencia directa: W3013726001 muestra que los criterios no son estables ni universales; una misma condicion puede interpretarse como barrera o ventaja segun actor y contexto, y que la decision real es iterativa, no lineal.",
    "Evidencia directa: W3177188553 aporta soporte para justificar adaptive reuse desde reduccion de GEI y conservacion de recursos, pero tambien advierte que los resultados LCA dependen fuertemente de limites del sistema, supuestos y datos de fondo.",
    "Inferencia prudente: para una tesis aplicada en Toronto, el corpus respalda mejor un marco de factibilidad multicriterio y comparado que una afirmacion concluyente de viabilidad economica o normativa local.",
    "Inferencia prudente: la sobre-elevacion en mass timber puede integrarse como alternativa de intervencion dentro de un marco de adaptive reuse, pero el corpus visible no valida por si solo su factibilidad estructural, normativa o financiera en Toronto.",
    "Inferencia prudente: el marco regenerativo/servicios ecosistemicos sirve mejor como complemento de criterios ambientales y urbanos de valor agregado que como nucleo metodologico principal del estudio."
  ],
  "evidence_gaps": [
    "Vacio bloqueante: no hay en el corpus visible evidencia local verificable sobre zoning, building code, fire code, permisos o politicas de conversion residencial en Toronto/Canada.",
    "Vacio bloqueante: no hay evidencia directa del corpus sobre mass timber rooftop additions en edificios comerciales B y C de Toronto ni sobre limites de altura, cargas, fuego, evacuacion o seguros.",
    "Vacio bloqueante: no hay datos locales de mercado para sustentar demanda, rentas, absorcion, CAPEX/OPEX, estructura financiera o modelo de negocio específico.",
    "Vacio importante: no aparece una tipologia local consolidada de edificios comerciales tipo B y C en Toronto con atributos geometricos, estructurales y de vacancia comparables.",
    "Vacio importante: faltan precedentes locales o regionales de oficina/comercial-a-vivienda con sobre-elevacion en madera masiva para triangulacion de criterios.",
    "Vacio importante: no hay protocolo local de evaluacion estructural para capacidad de carga remanente, refuerzo requerido y compatibilidad con sobre-elevacion liviana.",
    "Vacio importante: no hay validacion con actores locales (arquitectos, developers, code consultants, planners, lenders, insurers).",
    "Vacio menor pero relevante: el marco regenerativo no tiene evidencia empirica fuerte en este corpus para convertirse en criterio decisor principal de factibilidad."
  ],
  "proposal_directions": [
    "Adoptar una tesis de enfoque aplicado, no demostrativo-causal, orientada a construir un marco de factibilidad preliminar para reconversion habitacional con sobre-elevacion en mass timber.",
    "Estructurar la metodologia en fases: 1) revision sintesis del corpus y extraccion de criterios; 2) construccion de tipologias/casos-tipo; 3) matriz multicriterio de factibilidad; 4) contraste experto local; 5) aplicacion piloto a uno o varios prototipos.",
    "Tomar la vacancia/subuso como variable explicita del problema y del instrumento, no como supuesto binario.",
    "Distinguir entre escenarios de adaptive reuse parcial, mixto y de edificio completo, porque el corpus sugiere que esa diferencia cambia criterios y decisiones.",
    "Usar AHP o un MCDM simplificado para ponderar criterios, pero solo despues de una preseleccion cualitativa de criterios y alternativas relevantes para el caso.",
    "Incorporar una capa de prefactibilidad de carbono/beneficio ambiental basada en principios de whole-life thinking, evitando afirmaciones cuantitativas duras si no hay datos locales.",
    "Separar claramente factibilidad arquitectonica, regulatoria y de negocio, para que la tesis no dependa de probar cada una con el mismo nivel de evidencia.",
    "Tratar la sobre-elevacion en mass timber como alternativa de diseño-negocio a evaluar, no como solucion ya validada."
  ],
  "proposal_method_candidate": {
    "method_family": "MCDM aplicado con base cualitativa-comparada y validacion experta",
    "research_design": "Diseno secuencial mixto de alcance aplicado: revision sintetica del corpus -> analisis tipologico y de precedentes -> construccion de criterios -> ponderacion multicriterio preliminar (AHP o scoring ponderado) -> validac",
    "application_scope_status": "parcial",
    "candidate_techniques": [
      "Matriz de extraccion de criterios desde literatura priorizada.",
      "Analisis tipologico de edificios comerciales B y C candidatos.",
      "Revision comparada de precedentes de conversion/adaptive reuse y ampliacion vertical.",
      "Construccion de matriz de factibilidad por dimensiones: arquitectonica, regulatoria, ambiental y de negocio.",
      "AHP con comparaciones pareadas para ponderar criterios, sujeto a panel experto.",
      "Scoring multicriterio simplificado cuando no sea viable implementar AHP completo.",
      "Entrevistas semiestructuradas o panel Delphi corto con expertos locales para validar pesos y umbrales.",
      "Analisis de escenarios para alternativas de intervencion: reuse parcial, conversion completa, conversion + sobre-elevacion."
    ],
    "selection_rationale": [
      "El corpus ofrece apoyo directo a los modelos multicriterio y a la estructuracion de decisiones complejas con criterios heterogeneos.",
      "Tambien ofrece apoyo fuerte a un enfoque secuencial e iterativo, mas coherente con la naturaleza no lineal de estas decisiones.",
      "Permite traducir el problema de tesis a una herramienta util para decision preliminar, lo cual tiene alta utilidad academica y profesional.",
      "No exige afirmar causalidad ni contar con base de datos local completa desde el inicio; puede operar como marco tentativo que luego se valida.",
      "Es compatible con el interes del proyecto por criterios de evaluacion, factibilidad y formulacion de ideas de negocio."
    ],
    "evidence_support_level": "medio",
    "missing_validations": [
      "Validacion normativa local Toronto/Ontario/Canada.",
      "Validacion estructural específica para sobre-elevacion en mass timber sobre edificios comerciales existentes.",
      "Validacion economico-financiera con supuestos de mercado local.",
      "Validacion del panel de criterios y ponderaciones con actores locales.",
      "Prueba piloto en casos reales o prototipos locales con datos consistentes."
    ]
  },
  "proposal_framework_candidate": {
    "core_framework": "Marco de factibilidad multicriterio para adaptive reuse sostenible sensible a vacancia/subuso",
    "supporting_frameworks": [
      "Tipologia de intervencion de adaptive reuse: WBAR, MUML, PAR y STAR.",
      "Integracion holistica adaptive reuse + sostenibilidad.",
      "Whole-life thinking / LCA como modulo ambiental de apoyo, no como evidencia concluyente unica.",
      "Marco multiescalar de decision: edificio-activo-conjunto urbano.",
      "Servicios ecosistemicos/regenerative design como modulo optativo de valor urbano-ambiental."
    ],
    "selection_rationale": [
      "El framework de W4376279888 es el mas cercano al problema porque incorpora vacancia/subuso y tipos de reuse poco atendidos por la literatura.",
      "El marco de W3127169891 aporta una logica operativa para pasar de criterios abstractos a seleccion/ponderacion transparente.",
      "W3013726001 ayuda a evitar simplificaciones lineales y a reconocer que los criterios cambian segun escala, actor y contexto de desarrollo.",
      "W3177188553 fortalece la justificacion ambiental y la cautela metodologica respecto a carbono y comparaciones con obra nueva.",
      "W4200361813 puede enriquecer el discurso de valor urbano y regenerativo, pero no conviene sobredimensionarlo metodologicamente en esta tesis."
    ],
    "missing_validations": [
      "Aterrizaje del framework a edificios comerciales no patrimoniales B y C en Toronto.",
      "Incorporacion explicita de modulo regulatorio local.",
      "Incorporacion explicita de modulo de negocio con indicadores de mercado y riesgo.",
      "Validacion empírica de pesos, umbrales y reglas de descarte."
    ]
  },
  "gap_resolution_plan": {
    "inferable_with_care": [
      "Que un instrumento multicriterio es apropiado para evaluar compatibilidad y prefactibilidad de reuse cuando intervienen criterios heterogeneos.",
      "Que la vacancia/subuso debe modelarse como gradiente o condicion diferenciada y no como supuesto binario.",
      "Que los criterios deben agruparse al menos en dimensiones arquitectonicas, regulatorias/legales, ambientales y economicas/de negocio.",
      "Que la decision debe evaluarse por escenarios de intervencion, no solo por una opcion unica."
    ],
    "blocking_gaps": [
      "Parametros normativos de Toronto para conversion a vivienda y sobre-elevacion.",
      "Datos estructurales y de capacidad de edificios objetivo.",
      "Datos de mercado para el modelo de negocio.",
      "Precedentes locales verificables de mass timber overbuild en edificios comerciales existentes."
    ],
    "validation_actions": [
      "Recuperar y rehidratar fuentes locales de zoning by-law, Ontario Building Code/Toronto requirements y guias de office-to-residential conversion.",
      "Incorporar una ola LLM o revision experta dedicada exclusivamente a mass timber additions y requisitos de fire/structure/acoustics/egress.",
      "Construir una ficha de caso-tipo local con variables minimas: ano, luces, modulación, altura libre, sistema estructural, ocupacion, vacancia, lote, zoning.",
      "Realizar 5-10 entrevistas expertas o Delphi corto para validar criterios, pesos y umbrales de descarte.",
      "Preparar una prefactibilidad economica con escenarios y rangos, no con cifras puntuales no verificadas.",
      "Si es posible, aplicar el marco a 1-3 prototipos de edificios para probar consistencia y utilidad."
    ],
    "do_not_claim": [
      "No afirmar que la sobre-elevacion en mass timber ya es viable en Toronto sin validacion local.",
      "No afirmar que adaptive reuse siempre reduce carbono frente a obra nueva; el corpus muestra dependencia de escenarios y limites del sistema.",
      "No afirmar consenso universal sobre pesos de criterios; la evidencia muestra variacion entre actores y contextos.",
      "No usar el contexto del intake como cita academica.",
      "No presentar el marco regenerativo como evidencia empírica suficiente de factibilidad de negocio."
    ]
  },
